rationale: The only direct evidence is a single DOI-level reference match (reference_index 48) with no in-text citation context or unstructured reference text, so we cannot tell how substantively it is used. The cited paper is thematically relevant (causal/asymmetric peer effects and opinion leaders) to the citing paper’s peer-effects/network-activity focus, but current evidence does not show whether it is foundational versus a routine background mention.
